title: phanera khasiana var. tomentella
intro: Phanera khasiana var. tomentella is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. It is a type of tree that is native to the Himalayan region and is also known as the Himalayan ironwood. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains a high concentration of tannins and other bioactive compounds, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Phanera khasiana var. tomentella include reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial and fungal infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can be prepared into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments.
